title: Fried Pillowy Norwegian Red Snapper Paella
description: Cultured, pillowy, lazy-sunday Norwegian dish featuring red snapper, bonito-shave, and an egg-free twist
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the red snapper fillets and cook for 3 minutes on each side, until cooked through.
Remove the red snapper from the skillet and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the skillet and reduce heat to medium.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Add the paella rice and stir for 2 minutes to coat in oil and mix with onion.
Add the bonito flakes, saffron threads, soaked in hot water, sea salt, and black pepper to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Add 2 cups of fish stock to the skillet and bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low and cover the skillet with a tight-fitting lid.
Simmer for 18 minutes, until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Fluff the rice with a fork and stir in chopped parsley.
Transfer the rice mixture to a serving platter and top with the red snapper fillets.
Drizzle with remaining bonito flakes before serving.
